_note: Verified word glosses compiled from the phrasebook, the expressions and the lessons. Keys are FULLY VOCALIZED forms — there is deliberately no consonantal-skeleton fallback, see the header of tools/build-gloss.mjs. Generated; do not edit.

v.פִּיקַסוֹ: Pablo Picasso, Bathing sculpture in garden

v.הָעֲרוּצִים: channels, Rubinger covered every major moment for Time-Life
v.נֵגַטִיב: negative, physical-film term central to his archive
v.גוּפְעָנֵב: staged tableau, recurring Israeli photographic mode

v.פִיל: Phil, TLV stencil artist, multi-color cut-out method
v.גוֹלְדָה: Golda Meir, Souza portrait, frequently photographed
v.נוּעַ: Nova, recurring wall reference to Re\

v.תֵימָן: Yemen, origin of the filigree tradition
v.כְּתוּבָּה: ketubah, Jewish marriage contract, often illuminated
v.הַקַּרְדּוֹ: the Cardo, Old City Jerusalem, line of silver shops
v.זִהוּת: identity, major recurring subject (Mizrahi, queer, ethnic)
v.תוֹבְזָה: tovzah, rough-cut, hand-quarried look, common pre-1948
v.שָׁבוּ: shabu, fine "rocked" finish, traditional masonry detail
v.סַף: sill, window / door bottom in stone
v.מִשְׁקוֹף: lintel, top window / door beam, often stone
v.הַסִּינֵמָתֵיק: Tel Aviv Cinematheque, concrete cube on Sprinzak
v.גַּוּ: rib / fin, common brutalist surface element
v.בִּפַּלֵּשׁ: plastering-over, cosmetic damage to original surfaces
v.מָמִילָּא: Mamilla Mall, Safdie 2007, Old City Jerusalem
v.שַׁנְגְהָאי: Shanghai, Israeli architects active in China high-rise
v.מָמָ\"ד: mamad, reinforced safe room (1992 building code)
v.שְׁדֵרָה: boulevard, tree-lined median street, distinctive TLV form
v.בָּזֶלֶת: basalt, Israeli stone of the Galilee/Negev
v.אֵפֶר: ash, buried under the floor from death camps
v.הַחוּרְבָּה: the Hurva, main Old City Ashkenazi synagogue, rebuilt 2010
v.נִישָׁה: niche, common to all four traditions
v.אַנָּפָאזָה: Anaphaza (1993), Echad Mi Yodea section is famous
v.פֶלְדֶנְקְרַייז: Feldenkrais method, somatic relative of Gaga
v.יָתוֹמָה: orphan, Sara was raised in an orphanage in Jerusalem
v.מַעֲבָרָה: transit camp, early Mizrahi-immigrant absorption sites
v.שְׁבָזִי: Rabbi Shalom Shabazi, 17c poet, principal Diwan source
v.קִבּוּצִית: Kibbutz Contemporary Dance Company, frequent guest
v.הַגַ\\: the Jadam, informal Neve Tzedek café-meeting culture
v.בּוֹלְשׁוֹי: Bolshoi Ballet, many olim trained there
v.פּוּאַנְט: pointe, in Hebrew often "al ha-kchavim", "on points"
v.אִימְפַּלְסְטַנְץ: ImPulsTanz Vienna, major European partner festival
v.צַ\\: Charles Bronfman, Canadian donor of 2013 renovation

v.נוֹעַ: Noa (Achinoam Nini), singer, frequent Tzavta performer
v.שִׁירוֹן: songbook, printed lyrics for the audience
v.הַבָּארְבִּי: the Barby, flagship Israeli rock club
v.הַמַּתָּנָה: HaMatanah ("the Gift"), generic indie-band name pattern
v.הַתּוֹכְנִיָּה: the setlist / programme of the show
v.הִכְנַסְתָּ: "you got me in", popular crew slang for guest list
v.הוֹרְדוֹס: Herod, built the original Roman amphitheatre c. 22 BCE
v.שַׁבַּת: Sabbath, most venues do not run Friday-Saturday
v.לְכָבוֹד: To the honorable... (start of formal letter)
v.הָעֵמֶק: The Valley, Jezreel, kibbutzim, agricultural heart
v.שֶׁהֶחֱיָנוּ: who has kept us alive (new things, holidays)
